title: Gaming Report 2025
subtitle: Breaking boundaries to win
description: Bain's annual Video Game Consumption Survey — young gamers aged 2-17 across 399 respondents. Explore by age group, gamer type, device, and more.

agendas.name: Q37: Games Played in Last Year
agendas.summary: How many different games have respondents played? Distribution by age group and device.

charts.title: Number of games played — by age group
charts.note: % of respondents. Average: 5.8 games (2-12 y.o.) vs 5.6 games (13-17 y.o.)

charts.title: Number of games — by primary device (# respondents)
charts.note: # of respondents by device. Console gamers skew toward 10+ games (avg 6.3 games).

charts.title: Games played — immersive vs non-immersive
charts.note: % of respondents. Non-immersive gamers slightly more concentrated at 10+ (28% vs 23%).

agendas.name: Q38: Top Games Played
agendas.summary: Which games are played most often? Rankings across Rank 1, 2, and 3 picks.

charts.title: Top 10 games — rank distribution (% of respondents)
charts.note: Roblox leads Rank 1 at 16.5%, followed by Fortnite (11.7%) and Minecraft (10.3%).

charts.title: Top games — multi-device vs single-device players (%)
charts.note: Call of Duty has highest multi-device rate (56%); Free Fire is mostly single-device (88%).

agendas.name: Q39: When Started Playing
agendas.summary: How long have gamers been playing their most frequent game? Tenure by age and game title.

charts.title: Play tenure — by age group (%)
charts.note: 50% of 13-17 y.o. have been playing 2+ years vs 29% of 2-12 y.o.

charts.title: Top games — play tenure breakdown (%)
charts.note: Fortnite has highest long-tenure base (55% playing 2+ yrs). Candy Crush skews newer (only 15%).

agendas.name: Q40: Reasons for Playing
agendas.summary: Why do gamers play their most frequent game? Ranked reasons by design, social, brand, and platform.

charts.title: Top reasons — rank distribution (% of respondents)
charts.note: Gameplay is #1 (19% rank it first), friends (10%), immersive world (9%).

charts.title: Immersive vs non-immersive — % citing each reason (any rank)
charts.note: Immersive gamers value personalization (+16pp) and immersive worlds (+15pp). Non-immersive prioritize gameplay (+9pp) and characters (+12pp).

agendas.name: Q41: Hours Per Week
agendas.summary: Weekly time spent on most frequent game. Broken down by age and gamer type.

charts.title: Weekly hours — by age group (%)
charts.note: 13-17 y.o. play heavier: 17% play 11+ hrs/week vs 13% of 2-12 y.o. Average ~7 hrs/week.

charts.title: Weekly hours — immersive vs non-immersive (%)
charts.note: Immersive gamers play more: 49% play 6+ hrs/wk vs 37% non-immersive. Avg 7.2 vs 6.3 hrs.

agendas.name: Q42: Devices Used
agendas.summary: Multi-select device usage across consoles, mobile, PC, VR, and handhelds.

charts.title: Devices used for most frequent game (% of respondents)
charts.note: Multi-select (n=894, 1,295 total mentions). PlayStation leads at 29%. Avg 1.4 devices per gamer.
